title: Integrate Google Tag G-NQHZEVRPBL on the Zürich landing page

requirements.text: Inject Google Tag (gtag.js) with ID G-NQHZEVRPBL into the Zürich couples landing page (frontend/src/pages/ZurichCouplesLandingPage.tsx), scoped to that page only. Follow the same pattern used on the Basel couples landing page (BaselCouplesLandingPage.tsx), where a <script> tag loads https://www.googletagmanager.com/gtag/js?id=G-NQHZEVRPBL and an inline initialization script calls window.dataLayer and gtag('config', 'G-NQHZEVRPBL'). Use React Helmet or equivalent head-injection mechanism already in use on the Basel page.

quotes: Integriere den Google-Tag in die Zürich seite. G-NQHZEVRPBL

acceptanceCriteria: The gtag.js script with ID G-NQHZEVRPBL is loaded only when the Zürich couples landing page is rendered.
The gtag initialization script calls gtag('config', 'G-NQHZEVRPBL') on page load.
No other pages load this tag.
The implementation mirrors the pattern used in BaselCouplesLandingPage.tsx.

constraints: The Google Tag must be scoped exclusively to the Zürich landing page — do not add it globally or to any other page.
Do not modify frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or any files under frontend/src/components/ui.
nonGoals: Adding Google Tag to any other page (e.g., Basel, homepage, Swiss online page).
Creating a global tag manager setup.
